name: Sundowners Bar & Grill
description: Iconic West End waterfront restaurant and bar on Half Moon Bay Beach. Fresh seafood, Caribbean favorites, famous Monkey La La cocktails, and the best sunset views on Roatan. Live music Tue & Fri nights.
insiderTip: Grab a dock seat by 4:30pm for sunset.
addressDescription: Main road in West End, on the water

name: Cannibal Cafe
description: Best Mexican food on Roatan since 1996. Home of the legendary Big Kahuna Burrito, lionfish tacos, and a DIY salsa bar with custom spice levels. Huge portions, tiny prices — locals and divers pack this two-story West End spot nightly.
insiderTip: The banana pancakes are legendary.
addressDescription: Half Moon Bay road, West End

name: Roatan Oasis
description: Top-rated fine dining in West End with an internationally inspired, ever-changing menu. Steaks, fresh-caught seafood, and the island's largest craft beer selection. Stunning sunset views — reserve ahead, this intimate gem fills up fast.
insiderTip: Try the chipotle ribs and blue cheese gnocchi.

name: Calelu's
description: The baleada stand West End locals swear by. Giant fresh flour tortillas stuffed to order for under $2, plus a daily $6 lunch plate with meat, plantains, rice, and salad. Ocean-view patio upstairs — bring cash and an appetite.
insiderTip: Get the loaded baleada for under $2 - best deal on the island.

name: Anthony's Chicken
description: Rosie's legendary jerk chicken — custom-spiced to your heat level and served with her famous rice and beans. A $7 meal that rivals anything on the island. Cash only, made to order, worth every minute of the wait. A true West End institution.
insiderTip: The jerk chicken is a must - pair it with a cold local beer.

name: Pazzo Italian Restaurant
description: Tiny 25-seat Italian bistro where Chef Gianni handmakes every pasta dish to order. BYOB, reservations required 2+ weeks in advance — and absolutely worth the planning. Bruschetta and lasagna are the stuff of Roatan legend.
insiderTip: Reservations are essential - seating is very limited. Bring your own wine!
addressDescription: West End, near Half Moon Bay

name: Mila del Mar
description: Ranked #3 restaurant in West End on TripAdvisor. Upscale Caribbean dining on the second floor of The Beach House with unobstructed Half Moon Bay views. Locally caught fish, craft cocktails, and warm candlelit ambiance — pure island elegance.
insiderTip: Sunset dinner on the second floor terrace is unforgettable.
addressDescription: The Beach House, Half Moon Bay, West End

name: Blue Elephant Thai
description: Surprisingly authentic Thai cuisine overlooking the bay in West End. Two floors of seating plus waterfront tables. Pad thai, green curry, and spicy noodle dishes served with 16+ years of culinary expertise. A welcome change from Caribbean fare.
insiderTip: The pad thai with lobster is the splurge-worthy order.

name: Crisp Roatan
description: Roatan's go-to for fresh poke bowls, colorful salads, and cold-pressed juices in an Instagram-worthy setting. AC, WiFi, and vegan-friendly options make this cheerful West End cafe the perfect healthy lunch break between dives.
insiderTip: The poke bowls are fresh and perfectly portioned for a light lunch.

name: Sandy Buns Bakery
description: Voted #1 restaurant in West End by locals. Famous cinnamon rolls, Texas-style smoked brisket, and freshly baked breads by a former Texas couple. Gluten-free options with a dedicated fryer. Breakfast and lunch only — get there early.
insiderTip: Get there early - the fresh baked goods sell out fast.

name: Cafe de Palo
description: Hidden coffee gem in Mangrove Bight with three gorgeous seating areas — including a covered dock on the water with couches. Espresso, fresh pastries, all-day brunch, and cocktails. An escape from the West End crowds.
insiderTip: The atmosphere alone is worth the visit - perfect for remote work.

name: Thirsty Turtle
description: Lively beachfront bar and restaurant at Bananarama on West Bay Beach. Wood-fired brick oven pizza, sunset cocktails, and nightly entertainment — karaoke, trivia, crab races, and fire dancers. The fun never stops at the Turtle.
insiderTip: Their pizza spot on the deck is the perfect casual dinner.
hoursText: Daily breakfast, lunch, dinner; Happy Hour 4–6pm

name: Naboo Restaurant
description: Refined Caribbean fusion at this adults-only West Bay resort restaurant. Exquisite local cuisine, fresh seafood, and tropical garden ambiance with stunning ocean views. Surprisingly reasonable prices for the quality and setting.
insiderTip: Ask for the seafood special of the day.

name: Ibagari Sea Salt Restaurant
description: West Bay's #1-ranked restaurant on TripAdvisor. Chef Rick Zachrisson serves USDA Prime beef and fresh-caught fish in an intimate oceanfront setting at Ibagari Boutique Hotel. The lobster ravioli and limoncello tiramisu are legendary.
insiderTip: The tasting menu is a splurge but worth every penny.

name: Hangover Hut
description: Canadian-run West Bay beachfront spot famous for real-deal poutine, USDA burgers, donairs, and Philly cheese chicken. Live entertainment nightly. Comfort food done right on the Caribbean sand — because sometimes you need gravy with your ocean view.
insiderTip: Yes, the poutine in the Caribbean is actually amazing.

name: La Placita
description: Open-air collection of food stands in West Bay offering everything from pupusas to tacos to gelato. Multiple vendors, rock-bottom prices, and the casual street food vibe that makes Honduran eating so special. Perfect for a quick, cheap, authentic bite.
insiderTip: Try a pupusa from one stand and gelato from another - best cheap eats.

name: Vintage Pearl
description: French-Caribbean fine dining at Bananarama Resort with the island's largest wine cellar — a 6-page list. Walk barefoot down a tiki-lit sandy path to a candlelit table. The Sunday brunch is legendary. Worth every lempira.
insiderTip: The wine selection is unmatched on the island - ask the sommelier.
hoursText: Dinner nightly; Sunday brunch; Reservations recommended

name: Celeste's Island Cuisine
description: Award-winning West Bay seafood restaurant with the best lobster tail on the island and jumbo coconut shrimp. Wednesday all-you-can-eat lobster special is unmissable. Rescued parrots roam the garden. Outdoor romance meets Caribbean flavor.
insiderTip: Thursday night live music with lobster is the island's best date night.
hoursText: Daily lunch & dinner; Wed all-you-can-eat lobster

name: Dragonfly Sushi & Fusion
description: Fresh, creative sushi and Asian fusion at Coral Stone Plaza in Sandy Bay. Beautiful murals, full bar, and daily specials. Caribbean-Asian flavors meet affordable prices — a newer spot that's quickly earning a loyal following.
insiderTip: The specialty rolls are creative and beautifully presented.
addressDescription: Coral Stone Shopping Mall, Sandy Bay

name: Gio's Restaurant
description: World-renowned French Harbour landmark since 1994. Pioneer of the Spiny Caribbean King Crab dish and winner of Honduras' Top 30 Touristic Marvels award. Fresh seafood, Italian classics, and private waterfront dining at a Roatan institution.
insiderTip: The king crab is the reason this place exists - don't order anything else.

name: Romeo's Restaurant
description: Elegant waterfront Italian dining in French Harbour since 1978. Wood-fired pizza from a proper oven, USDA choice steaks, a fresh herb garden, and an excellent wine list. One of Roatan's oldest and most respected restaurants.
insiderTip: The wood-fired pizza rivals anything in Italy.

name: Herby's Sports Bar & Grill
description: Arguably the best sports bar in Honduras. 34 HD TVs, a 10-foot screen, sports memorabilia museum, and a suspended scoreboard — all inside the Clarion Suites. Wings, pizza from $6, and ice-cold beers. NFL Sundays are electric.
insiderTip: Come for game day - the wings and atmosphere can't be beat.

name: Salty Dawg
description: Lively Cajun-inspired waterfront eatery in Oak Ridge, serving creative shrimp, fish, chicken, and pork dishes bursting with flavor. Shares a building on the bay with Serve Coffee. Live music every Friday afternoon draws a fun crowd. A real east-side gem worth the drive from West Bay.
insiderTip: Come for the Friday live music from 1-4pm — grab a waterfront seat early and try the Cajun shrimp.
addressDescription: On the water in downtown Oak Ridge, same building as Serve Coffee

name: Booty Bar
description: Waterfront bar by day, pumping nightclub after dark. Two buildings over the water with DJs, light shows, and Caribbean cocktails. West End's go-to spot when you want to dance with your feet in the sand.
insiderTip: It's chill during the day but gets wild after 10pm.

name: Frank's
description: Late-night West End institution with a cigar lounge, outdoor dance floor between two buildings, and premium Cuban and Honduran cigars. Excellent cocktails flow until the last person leaves. The place to end your night in West End.
insiderTip: This is where the night ends up - usually after midnight.

name: Tequila Jacks
description: Craft cocktails, tequila flights, and handmade mojitos at the Brisas del Mar hotel pool bar. Friday night bingo, live bands, and their famous Monkey La La keep the crowd coming back. West End's hottest new gathering spot.
insiderTip: The tequila flight is the best way to start your evening.
addressDescription: Brisas del Mar Hotel, West End
hoursText: Daily; Happy Hour, Fri bingo, live bands

name: Bariloche
description: New wine bar and steakhouse with generous grilled meat platters perfect for sharing. Live music Wednesday and Friday nights, plus a beach bar out front for sunset cocktails. West End's newest chill-and-grill spot.
insiderTip: Wednesday and Friday are live music nights - arrive early for a good seat.

name: Blue Marlin
description: Roatan's most popular dance club, completely refurbished in 2022. Legendary Thursday karaoke, Saturday trivia nights, and a pumping dance floor with generous rum punches. Breakfast by day, mayhem by night — a true West End tradition.
insiderTip: This is THE party spot past 10pm - the dance floor gets packed.

name: Eagle Ray's Bar
description: Laid-back waterfront dive bar built out over the Caribbean, popular with expats and the diver crowd. Cold beers, excellent fish tacos, and a fish soup loaded with crab and lobster. No frills, just good vibes and ocean breezes.
insiderTip: The cheapest cold beers in West End - perfect after a dive.

name: Happy Harry's
description: Popular seafood restaurant and bar with karaoke nights that draw a fun crowd. Gr...

name: Beachers Bar & Grill
description: Family-run for 15+ years, directly on the white sand of West Bay Beach. The island's longest happy hour (10am–6pm, 2-for-1 cocktails), famous coconut shrimp, and Monday lobster specials — 3 tails for ~$20. Chairs, sun, cold drinks, paradise.
insiderTip: Sunday Fun-Day is the best day party on the island.
addressDescription: On the sand, West Bay Beach

name: San Simon Beach Club
description: West Bay's most exclusive beach club with beachside bars, fusion cuisine, lounging beds, and Roatan's premier wedding venue. Barefoot luxury on white sand with genius cocktails and the Caribbean at your feet. Day passes available.
insiderTip: Get a day pass and enjoy the lounge chairs with bottle service.

name: Coconut Bar
description: Where Coxen Hole locals actually drink. Cold beer, local rum, no tourists, no pretense. If you want to experience real island life beyond the resorts, pull up a stool at this no-frills neighborhood bar.
insiderTip: This is where the locals go - great for an authentic experience.

name: Roatan Island Brewing Co.
description: Roatan's only craft brewery, tucked in lush Palmetto Bay valley. Tropical ales, lagers, apple cider, kombucha, and gluten-free options. Nature trails, foosball, ping pong, fire pit, and cold pints — worth the drive off the beaten path.
insiderTip: Try the West End IPA - brewed with local ingredients.

name: Paya Bay Beach Bar
description: Secluded bohemian beach bar at the island's quiet east end, perched on a bluff above Bliss Beach. 24-hour bar, pristine sand, zero crowds, and a clothing-optional vibe that's not for everyone — but unforgettable if it's your scene.
insiderTip: It's worth the drive east for a completely different Roatan experience.

name: Coconut Tree Divers
description: PADI 5-Star Instructor Development Center and Roatan's only TEC Diving Center. IDC, rebreather, sidemount, and divemaster internship programs with 40+ years combined experience. Half Moon Bay's premier dive operation.
insiderTip: Book the early morning dive for the calmest water and best visibility.

name: Roatan Divers
description: Multi-award-winning PADI 5-Star IDC Eco Center with a fleet of 4 fast custom boats and private docks. Boutique diving focused on small groups, personalized service, and environmental responsibility. The gold standard for Roatan diving.
insiderTip: Their eco-friendly approach means healthier reefs and better dives.

name: Sun Divers Roatan
description: Roatan's first-ever PADI Eco Center, run by husband-wife team Shannon and Natalie. Concierge-style service, active coral restoration programs, and a deep commitment to reef conservation. Diving with a purpose on the Mesoamerican Barrier Reef.
insiderTip: The small group size means more personal attention and better dives.

name: West End Divers
description: The longest continuously operating dive shop in West End — a PADI 5-Star Dive Center since 1991. The only shop offering blackwater dive service on Roatan. Spacious boats, well-maintained gear, and decades of reef knowledge.
insiderTip: Their experience shows - the dive masters know every reef like the back of their hand.

name: Native Sons Diving
description: Founded in 1993 by local dive legend Alvin Jackson — the longest same-owner dive shop on Roatan. Boutique groups on his custom-built 48ft Uba Isti boat, valet diving, and 60+ years of intimate reef knowledge. A spotless safety record and a true island original.
insiderTip: Ask Alvin for stories - he's been diving these reefs for 60 years.

name: West Bay Divers
description: PADI 5-Star Dive Center with two locations right on West Bay Beach. 100+ years combined staff experience, 15 PADI specialty courses, and direct access to the world's second-largest barrier reef. Perfect for beginners and pros alike.
insiderTip: Shore diving right off West Bay Beach is incredible for beginners.

name: Island Snorkel Tours
description: Guided snorkel tours exploring the reef right off West Bay Beach — no diving experience needed. Tropical fish, sea turtles, eagle rays, and vibrant coral just 30 feet from shore. The easiest way to see Roatan's underwater world.
insiderTip: Great for kids who want to see the reef without scuba gear.

name: Deep Blue Freediving
description: Explore Roatan's reef on a single breath. Professional PADI and Apnea Total freediving instruction from beginner to master level. Warm crystal-clear water, great depths close to shore, and zero currents — ideal conditions for the ultimate underwater experience.
insiderTip: Even the beginner course is a life-changing experience.

name: Paradise Beach Dive Center
description: PADI 5-Star Instructor Development Center at Paradise Beach Hotel on West Bay Beach. Full range of courses and daily dives.
insiderTip: Their Discover Scuba program is one of the best intro experiences.

name: Mayan Divers at Mayan Princess
description: Dive shop at the Mayan Princess Resort offering convenient reef access and PADI courses for resort guests and walk-ins.
insiderTip: If you're staying at Mayan Princess, the dive shop is right downstairs.

name: Oak Ridge Snorkel Adventure
description: Guided snorkel tours through the magical mangrove tunnels and reefs around Oak Ridge — Roatan's 'Venice.' See incredible birdlife, stilt houses, and untouched reef systems. Nothing else like it on the island. A true hidden gem.
insiderTip: The mangrove snorkel is a completely different world from the reef.

name: West Bay Beach Bonfire
description: Beach bonfire nights on West Bay Beach with fire dancers, acoustic music, and dancing under the stars. Lobster bakes and BBQ beside crackling flames on white sand. The kind of night you came to the Caribbean for.
insiderTip: The fire dancers are mesmerizing - find a spot in the sand early.

name: RoaCrawl Bar Crawl
description: Organized bar crawl through West End's best nightlife spots with guides, drink specials at each stop, and round-trip transport. Hits Frank's, Booty Bar, and more. $169/person for the ultimate insider's night out — the fastest way to experience it all.
insiderTip: Book in advance - it's the best way to experience West End nightlife in one night.

name: Roots Reggae Bar
description: Authentic reggae bar in West End with Garifuna music, local DJs, and a true Caribbean groove. No cover, cold beers, and a dance floor that feels like the real island. Come for the music, stay for the vibe.
insiderTip: This is where you'll find the most authentic island music.

name: Punta Gorda Garifuna Nights
description: Authentic Garifuna drumming and cultural performances in the traditional village of Punta Gorda. Two-hour shows with traditional dinner under thatch palapas. UNESCO-recognized intangible heritage — a soul-stirring experience you won't find in any resort.
insiderTip: This is the real deal - authentic Garifuna culture you won't find anywhere else.

name: Bodden Tours
description: The original Best of Roatan tour operator since 2003. Native guide Victor Bodden offers private island tours, Zip-n-Dip excursions, snorkel trips, ATV adventures, and wheelchair-accessible tours. From $30/person — nobody knows this island better.
insiderTip: The Best of Roatan combo tour is the most popular for a reason.
addressDescription: Meeting point at cruise port, Coxen Hole

name: Gumbalimba Park
description: 20-acre beachfront nature park in West Bay with Capuchin monkeys, macaws, exotic birds, a cave tour, and a 12-cable zipline course with suspension bridges. Immaculate grounds and experienced guides make this Roatan's must-visit family attraction.
insiderTip: Feed the monkeys and parrots - they're incredibly friendly.

name: Daniel Johnson's Monkey & Sloth Hangout
description: Beloved animal sanctuary in French Cay where you can hold sloths, play with capuchin monkeys, and meet scarlet macaws up close. A rescue and rehabilitation center with respectful, personal animal encounters — the island's most heartwarming experience.
insiderTip: Note: since 2025 guests can no longer hold sloths, but you can still get close.
addressDescription: French Cay, 25 min from cruise port

name: South Shore Canopy Zipline
description: Adrenaline-pumping zipline park with 11 cables, 12 platforms, and a 1,300-foot longest line across 28 acres of eco-park. Speeds up to 40 mph, 100 feet above the canopy. 45-minute guided tours with cultural narration. $65–$115.
insiderTip: The Superman line is the longest at 1,300 feet - pure adrenaline.

name: Ruthless Roatan Charters
description: Deep sea and reef fishing charters from Half Moon Bay — blue marlin, sailfish, mahi-mahi, and wahoo. All trips include catered appetizers, cold beverages, and fresh tuna sashimi if caught. Vintage luxury boats with shade and indoor seating.
insiderTip: The half-day reef fishing trip is great for beginners.

name: Roatan Chocolate Factory
description: Bean-to-bar chocolate tour using 100% organic Honduran cacao. Watch the full process, sample 12 varieties, or take a hands-on chocolate-making class. Founded 2010 on West End's main street — sweet, educational, and uniquely Roatan.
insiderTip: The tasting at the end is generous - save room.

name: Roatan Rum Company
description: Craft rum distillery between West End and West Bay with free tastings and rum cake samples. The 'Blendatorium' lets you create your own custom rum from 50+ natural extracts ($29–$39). Caribbean Sea views and a fusion bar. A must-stop.
insiderTip: The aged rum tasting flight is worth every penny.

name: Mangrove Kayak Tours
description: Guided kayak tours through the magical mangrove tunnels of Oak Ridge, Roatan's 'Venice.' Paddle past stilt houses, spot incredible birdlife, and explore untouched waterways. Life jackets and snorkel gear included. A peaceful adventure off the tourist trail.
insiderTip: The morning tour has the best light and wildlife activity.

name: Horseback Riding Roatan
description: Beach horseback riding along Sandy Bay's scenic shores with mangrove and ironshore views. Walk, trot, or gallop over secluded sand with the Caribbean on one side and palms on the other. From $55/person — perfect for first-timers and experienced riders.
insiderTip: The sunset beach ride is the most romantic option.

name: Punta Gorda Cultural Tour
description: Authentic Garifuna cultural experience in Honduras' oldest village, founded 1797. Traditional punta drumming, local cuisine (Machuca, yucca bread), and Guifity herbal beverages. UNESCO-recognized heritage brought to life by the community that keeps it alive.
insiderTip: The cassava bread making demonstration is fascinating.

name: Little French Key
description: TripAdvisor's #1-ranked attraction in Roatan. Private island paradise with white sand, in-water palapa tables, a mini zoo, snorkeling, and the underwater Mayatlantis museum. Zero carbon footprint — wind power, rainwater collection. From $118 including lunch, drinks, and transport.
insiderTip: Book the VIP cabana for the ultimate private island experience.
hoursText: Daily; 3–5 min boat ride from French Harbour

name: Macaw Container Market
description: Vibrant shopping village built from recycled shipping containers, 200m east of the airport. Locally made crafts, a brewery, chocolate factory, coffee shop, restaurants, and a liquor store — all supporting Honduran artisans. Free admission, unique vibe.
insiderTip: Browse the handmade jewelry stalls - great prices for unique pieces.

name: Town Center at Port of Roatan
description: Duty-free shopping mall at the Port of Roatan cruise terminal. Jewelry, cigars, chocolate, souvenirs, and local artisan gifts spread across 6.5 acres. Serves 580,000+ cruise passengers annually — your first taste of the island.
insiderTip: Duty-free prices on jewelry are actually decent deals.

name: Coxen Hole Municipal Market
description: The real local market in Coxen Hole — fresh produce, Garifuna crafts, Lenca pottery, and street food. Recently upgraded with modern facilities and organized stalls. Skip the cruise port shops and come here for authentic Honduran goods at local prices.
insiderTip: This is where locals shop - great for authentic souvenirs at local prices.

name: Waves of Art
description: Eye-catching folk art gallery in West End supporting fair trade organizations and local artisan co-ops. Custom framing, watercolors, fine art, and unique handmade gifts. Colorful, curated, and deeply connected to Roatan's creative community.
insiderTip: The recycled art pieces made from ocean debris are incredible.

name: Rusty Fish Recycled Island Art
description: Beautiful art made from ocean debris and recycled materials collected around Roatan. Founded by Adam Hunt, benefiting 60 local families. Metal pieces, mosaics, and windchimes — each one unique, meaningful, and impossible to find anywhere else.
insiderTip: Every piece has a story - ask the artists about their process.

name: Wild Roatan
description: Conservation-focused West End shop selling eco-friendly clothing, reef-safe sunscreen, batana oil, natural soaps, and island-branded gear. Proceeds fund wildlife conservation and beach cleanup projects. Shop with purpose.
insiderTip: Every purchase supports reef conservation efforts.

name: Driftwood Designs
description: Handcrafted home decor and gifts made from local driftwood, shells, and natural materials. A West End treasure trove of island-made items you won't find at the cruise port. Perfect for a meaningful souvenir.
insiderTip: The driftwood lamps are stunning conversation pieces.

name: Caribbean Cigar Co.
description: Hand-rolled Honduran cigars in a relaxed West Bay lounge. Premium Cuban and Honduran selections, comfortable seating, and ocean breezes. The perfect post-dinner ritual for cigar lovers on vacation.
insiderTip: Watch them roll cigars by hand - then smoke one on the deck.

name: West Bay Beach Gift Shop
description: Beachside souvenir shop with t-shirts, magnets, postcards, and beach accessories right on West Bay.
insiderTip: Grab a Roatan t-shirt and reef-safe sunscreen before hitting the beach.

name: Honduras Coffee Co.
description: Premium Honduran single-origin coffee beans, gift sets, and brewing gear. Tours on coffee growing, harvesting, and roasting. Take home the taste of Honduras — some of the best specialty coffee in Central America, direct from the source.
insiderTip: Buy a bag of beans to take home - Honduran coffee is world-class.

name: Infinity Bay Resort
description: Premier West Bay resort with beachfront condos, multiple pools, a full dive center, and direct snorkeling from shore — swim with sea turtles steps from your room. Free breakfast, complimentary kayaks, and the island's most famous beach at your doorstep.
insiderTip: Book an oceanfront room for direct beach access and reef snorkeling.

name: Kimpton Grand Roatan
description: West Bay's most upscale hotel — 122 rooms, four world-class restaurants, a tranquil spa, and a beachside infinity pool on the world's second-largest barrier reef. Couples rate it 9.4/10. Modern luxury meets Caribbean soul. From $181/night.
insiderTip: The rooftop bar has the best panoramic views of any hotel on the island.

name: Arca Roatan
description: Intimate luxury boutique with only 16 oceanfront rooms — each with king bed, indoor/outdoor showers, and private terrace. Saltwater pool, beachfront restaurant Ahari, and full concierge. Ranked #4 hotel in Bay Islands on TripAdvisor.
insiderTip: With only 16 rooms it feels like your own private resort.

name: Bananarama Dive Resort
description: Budget-friendly beachfront dive resort on West Bay Beach with hammocks on every porch, free breakfast, and a PADI dive center. Day passes just $30. The social hub where divers, backpackers, and sun-seekers converge. From $99/night.
insiderTip: Best budget option directly on West Bay Beach.

name: The Beach House
description: Intimate 14-room beachfront boutique hotel considered the best place to stay in West End. Overlooking Half Moon Bay with AC suites, daily made-to-order breakfast, and the acclaimed Mila del Mar restaurant downstairs. From $237/night.
insiderTip: Spring for the oceanview room - Half Moon Bay is your front yard.

name: Tranquilseas Eco Lodge & Dive Center
description: Ranked #1 eco lodge on Roatan with a 9.3/10 on Booking.com. PADI dive resort in Sandy Bay with The Sunken Fish restaurant, outdoor pool, and the friendliest staff on the island. Peaceful, personal, sustainable. From $170/night.
insiderTip: The rooftop sunset terrace is the best spot in Sandy Bay.

name: Anthony's Key Resort
description: Iconic Sandy Bay resort since 1968 — waterfront bungalows, PADI 5-Star dive center, and Atlantic bottlenose dolphin encounters in a natural lagoon. Kayaks, paddleboards, horseback riding, and three daily meals included. Staff remembers your name.
insiderTip: The dolphin dive is a once-in-a-lifetime experience.

name: Roatan Backpackers' Hostel
description: Sandy Bay hostel with dorms from $18/night, private rooms, a pool with waterfall, and communal kitchen. Near Anthony's Key dolphins and botanical gardens. Owner Mel makes everyone feel at home. The social hub for budget travelers on Roatan.
insiderTip: The pool area is where you'll meet fellow travelers.

name: CoCo View Resort
description: Legendary south shore dive resort — the most returned-to in the world. Shore diving 24/7, 150+ dive sites, Mary's Place (voted Best Dive Site 2018), and the Prince Albert wreck. Custom 50-foot boats and a PADI 5-Star dive shop.
insiderTip: The wall dive right off the resort is world-famous.

name: Camp Bay Eco Lodge
description: Remote eco-lodge on Roatan's quiet east end — solar-powered, beachfront, and surrounded by wild horses, birds, and iguanas. The island's best kitesurfing, fly fishing for bonefish and tarpon, and pristine reef snorkeling. From $120/night. Truly off the grid.
insiderTip: If you want to truly disconnect, this is the place.

name: Captain Van's Rentals
description: Roatan's most popular scooter and motorcycle rental since 1997. Yamaha 115cc scooters from $35/day, 155cc from $55/day, plus Honda motorcycles and bicycles. West Bay Mall location. Must be 18+ with valid license and riding experience.
insiderTip: The scooter is the best way to explore the island on your own.

name: Roatan PaddleSports Rentals
description: Paddleboard and kayak rentals from the West End waterfront at Half Moon Bay. Hourly, daily, weekly, and monthly rates. Professional instruction available. Two locations — the easiest way to get on the water and explore the coastline.
insiderTip: Sunrise paddleboarding in Half Moon Bay is pure magic.

name: Reef Rider Jet Ski Rentals
description: Jet ski rentals on West Bay Beach — $85 for 30 minutes, accommodates 2 riders. Guided tours and free-ride options with Yamaha jet skis. Fuel, insurance, and life jackets included. Minimum age 6 (passenger), 14 (driver with parent).
insiderTip: The guided jet ski tour to the shipwreck is a blast.

name: Roatan Ferry Terminal (Galaxy Wave)
description: Ferry service connecting Roatan to La Ceiba on the mainland via the Galaxy Wave — a 152-foot catamaran carrying 460 passengers. 75-minute crossing, modern Easy Ticket kiosks, free parking, and cafeteria onboard. Your gateway to the mainland.
insiderTip: Book the early morning ferry for the calmest crossing.

name: West Bay Water Taxi
description: Water taxi between West End and West Bay Beach — a scenic 10-minute ride through the lagoon for about $3. The fun, fast alternative to the road. Minimum 3 passengers per trip. Catch one at Infinity Bay or Foster's dock.
insiderTip: Only $3-5 per person and way more fun than driving.
addressDescription: West End dock to West Bay Beach

name: West Bay Beach
description: Roatan's most famous beach — #1 in Central America and ranked 9th globally. Powdery white sand, crystal-clear turquoise water, and the Mesoamerican Barrier Reef just offshore. Restaurants, bars, water sports, and loungers line the shore. The Caribbean dream, realized.
insiderTip: Walk to the far south end for a quieter experience away from the resorts.

name: Half Moon Bay Beach
description: Beautiful crescent-shaped beach in the heart of West End with white sand, turquoise water, and reef snorkeling right from shore. Palm-fringed, calm, and walkable from every bar and dive shop in the village. The quintessential West End experience.
insiderTip: The crescent shape protects the bay - great for calm swimming.

name: West End Beach
description: Mile-long relaxed beach along the West End strip, popular with backpackers and divers. Budget accommodations, dive shops, and bars line the shore. Reef close enough to swim to. The laid-back, no-frills side of Roatan's main tourist village.
insiderTip: Rent a kayak and paddle along the coastline at sunset.
addressDescription: Along the main road, West End

name: Tabyana Beach
description: Rated among the best beaches in the Caribbean with the reef just 70 feet from shore. White sand, crystal-clear water, excellent snorkeling, kayaking, and banana boats. Organized chairs, umbrellas, restrooms, and a restaurant. Popular cruise excursion destination.
insiderTip: The reef is so close you can snorkel to it in minutes.
addressDescription: West Bay, near the south end

name: Camp Bay Beach
description: The longest completely natural beach on Roatan — 2.5 miles of pristine white sand on the quiet northeast coast. Gentle waves, zero crowds, and untouched beauty. Bring your own everything. The island's best-kept secret for tranquility seekers.
insiderTip: Worth the drive for a true deserted beach experience.
addressDescription: Camp Bay, east end of island

name: Beach Club Roatan (Palm Beach)
description: Spacious 150,000 sq ft private beach club on the north shore at Johnson Bight. Horseback riding, snorkeling, jet skiing, kayaking, and Lionfish Louie's restaurant. Day passes include roundtrip transport from cruise ports. 1.5 miles from Mahogany Bay.
insiderTip: The horseback ride on the beach is unforgettable.

name: Punta Gorda Beach
description: Beach at Honduras' oldest Garifuna settlement, founded ~1797. Cultural significance meets Caribbean sand. Traditional huts, drum ceremonies, and yucca bread alongside gentle waves. A living connection to Roatan's Afro-Caribbean heritage — not just a beach, a story.
insiderTip: Combine a beach day with a Garifuna cultural tour.

name: Mahogany Bay Beach
description: Manmade beach at the Carnival cruise port with 20 acres to explore, lounge chairs, food, and water activities.
insiderTip: If you're on a cruise, the beach pass is the easiest option.
